license_note: Apache-2.0 candidate; attribution obligations must be reviewed before any bundle.
offline_note: Runs locally after an already-available package is detected; no network probe is allowed.
maintenance_note: Focused Python helper with current PyPI presence; still treated as optional.
language_coverage_note: Unicode repair is language-agnostic and useful before language detection.
reason: Lowest-risk cleanup candidate for mojibake and Unicode repair before heavier language providers.

license_note: Babel source license plus Unicode license for bundled CLDR-derived locale data.
offline_note: Can use packaged locale data when already present; no locale-data download is allowed.
maintenance_note: Established Python locale helper; candidate probe only checks local availability.
language_coverage_note: Useful for dates, numbers, territories, and locale display data across first-class languages.
reason: Low-risk locale helper that improves normalization without translation or pronunciation claims.

license_note: Unicode license family; exact binding and data license must be checked per local package.
offline_note: May be available through OS or language bindings, but this cycle does not inspect system packages.
maintenance_note: Standards-backed data source, but direct integration surface is broader than Babel.
language_coverage_note: Broad locale data source; defer direct use until a local binding policy exists.
reason: Keep as a standards reference while probing Babel first as the smaller local surface.

offline_note: Designed for offline use after the package is already available locally.
maintenance_note: Current PyPI presence and active language-detection focus; still optional.
language_coverage_note: Candidate for first-class language detection, including short and mixed-language text checks.
reason: First language-detection probe candidate after cleanup and locale helpers.

license_note: Google CLD3 source license must be verified with the local binding.
offline_note: Local-only if an existing binding is present; no build or dependency fetch is allowed.
maintenance_note: Packaging and binding health varies by ecosystem.
language_coverage_note: Language detection candidate, but short-span and binding behavior need evidence.
reason: Keep as degraded until a local binding proves availability without setup side effects.

license_note: fastText and model artifact licenses must be reviewed separately.
offline_note: Model-backed and only acceptable when the model already exists locally.
maintenance_note: Model artifact policy is unresolved for TES.
language_coverage_note: Broad language identification model, but artifact size and coverage policy are out of scope.
reason: Defer until model artifact acquisition and storage policy is explicitly approved.

license_note: Project and language-package licenses must be reviewed separately before any use.
offline_note: Offline translation requires local language packages; this cycle forbids downloading them.
maintenance_note: Viable local translation candidate, but model management is a separate policy surface.
language_coverage_note: Coverage depends on installed language pairs and pivot paths; no pair is certified here.
reason: Defer to SPEC-007 because translation needs protected-term and no-summary fixture proof.

license_note: GPL-3.0-or-later core with additional repository licenses; bundling needs owner review.
offline_note: System-local if already installed; no package manager probe or installation is allowed.
maintenance_note: Mature open-source synthesizer, but adapter use must not become provider certification.
language_coverage_note: Broad language/accent support; per-language voice quality remains unverified.
reason: Defer to SPEC-008 pronunciation provider boundary.

license_note: GPLv3+ candidate; backend licenses must also be reviewed.
offline_note: May require an already-installed backend such as eSpeak NG.
maintenance_note: Useful wrapper surface, but backend coupling creates setup risk.
language_coverage_note: Coverage follows backend and selected language switch behavior.
reason: Defer until eSpeak NG boundary and license posture are settled.

license_note: MIT package license with per-language lexicon/model data to review.
offline_note: Local lexicon and G2P model use only when already available.
maintenance_note: Useful but language-pack and Python-version posture need local evidence.
language_coverage_note: Includes several first-class languages but not the full target set, especially Hebrew.
reason: Classify degraded until coverage and per-language data licenses are proven locally.

license_note: MIT candidate according to published project material.
offline_note: Local-only if package and language tables are already present.
maintenance_note: Research-grade G2P surface; maintenance and script coverage need local evidence.
language_coverage_note: Broad but language-script-specific; exact target-language coverage is not certified.
reason: Keep degraded until target language-script pairs are proven by fixtures.

license_note: Same eSpeak NG GPL-3.0-or-later posture applies.
offline_note: Only local system availability may be inspected.
maintenance_note: Hebrew behavior must be reviewed separately from broad eSpeak support.
language_coverage_note: Hebrew remains explicitly degraded until pronunciation quality is proven.
reason: Preserve the ADR posture that Hebrew enrichment is quality-sensitive and not certified.

license_note: Hebrew-specific code, model, and dataset licenses must be reviewed separately.
offline_note: Model/data use would require local artifacts, which are forbidden in this cycle.
maintenance_note: New Hebrew-specific research candidate; owner review is required before use.
reason: Defer until Hebrew policy, dataset licensing, and model artifact handling are approved.

license_note: Apache-2.0 project license, but dependency stack must be reviewed before any future reconsideration.
offline_note: No local setup or heavyweight dependency probing is allowed in the current skill scope.
maintenance_note: Advanced WFST package with platform and dependency constraints.
language_coverage_note: Text-normalization coverage is beyond the minimal reactive skill boundary.
reason: Reject for the current `tes-tts` convergence scope because it is too heavyweight and platform-sensitive.
